name: ios-ssl-kill-switch
summary: Blackbox tool to disable SSL certificate validation - including certificate pinning - within iOS Apps.

documentation.readme: Blackbox tool to disable SSL certificate validation - including certificate pinning - within iOS Apps.
usage_sections: v0.5: Complete rewrite in order to add support for proxy-ing Apple's App Store application.
v0.3: Bug fixes and support for iOS 6.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with nabla-c0d3/ssl-kill-switch2 (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with ios-Hack/ios-pentest-tools (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with garryknight/ios-spy (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the iOS TLS pinning bypass (older) category. Fits when the engagement needs: Blackbox tool to disable SSL certificate validation - including certificate pinning - within iOS Apps.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Blackbox tool to disable SSL certificate validation - including certificate pinning - within iOS Apps.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: brew install dpkg. Blackbox tool to disable SSL certificate validation - including certificate pinning - within iOS Apps.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases, and licence before use. Operates against the operator's own authorized scope only.

use_cases: When you need iOS TLS pinning bypass (older), use ios-ssl-kill-switch because Blackbox tool to disable SSL certificate validation - including certificate pinning - within iOS Apps.
When chaining iOS TLS pinning bypass (older) tools, pair ios-ssl-kill-switch with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `ios-ssl-kill-switch --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
